skills.en: Instructing the agent to ask for additional identifiers when tool results return multiple matches, rather than selecting based on heuristics
skills.sr: Uputstvo agentu da traži dodatne identifikatore kada alat vrati više poklapanja, umesto biranja po heuristici

title.en: Implement error propagation strategies across multi-agent systems
title.sr: Implementacija strategija propagacije grešaka kroz multi-agent sisteme

knowledge.en: Structured error context (failure type, attempted query, partial results, alternative approaches) as enabling intelligent coordinator recovery decisions
knowledge.sr: Strukturisan kontekst greške (tip otkaza, pokušani upit, parcijalni rezultati, alternativni pristupi) kao osnova za inteligentne odluke koordinatora o oporavku

knowledge.en: The distinction between access failures (timeouts needing retry decisions) and valid empty results (successful queries with no matches)
knowledge.sr: Razlika između grešaka pristupa (timeout-i koji traže odluku o ponavljanju) i validnih praznih rezultata (uspešni upiti bez pogodaka)

knowledge.en: Why generic error statuses ("search unavailable") hide valuable context from the coordinator
knowledge.sr: Zašto generički status greške („search unavailable") sakriva vredan kontekst od koordinatora

knowledge.en: Why silently suppressing errors (returning empty results as success) or terminating entire workflows on single failures are both anti-patterns
knowledge.sr: Zašto su i tiho gušenje grešaka (vraćanje praznih rezultata kao uspeha) i obaranje celog toka na jedan otkaz oba anti-paterni

skills.en: Returning structured error context including failure type, what was attempted, partial results, and potential alternatives to enable coordinator recovery
skills.sr: Vraćanje strukturisanog konteksta greške: tip otkaza, šta je pokušano, parcijalni rezultati i moguće alternative radi oporavka koordinatora

skills.en: Distinguishing access failures from valid empty results in error reporting so the coordinator can make appropriate decisions
skills.sr: Razlikovanje grešaka pristupa od validnih praznih rezultata u izveštavanju o greškama da koordinator donese ispravnu odluku

skills.en: Having subagents implement local recovery for transient failures and only propagate errors they cannot resolve, including what was attempted and partial results
skills.sr: Da subagenti implementiraju lokalni oporavak za tranzijentne otkaze i prosleđuju samo greške koje ne mogu rešiti, uz ono što je pokušano i parcijalne rezultate

skills.en: Structuring synthesis output with coverage annotations indicating which findings are well-supported versus which topic areas have gaps due to unavailable sources
skills.sr: Strukturiranje izlaza sinteze sa anotacijama pokrivenosti koje ukazuju koji nalazi su dobro potkrepljeni a koje teme imaju rupe zbog nedostupnih izvora

title.en: Manage context effectively in large codebase exploration
title.sr: Efikasno upravljanje kontekstom u istraživanju velikih kodnih baza

knowledge.en: Context degradation in extended sessions: models start giving inconsistent answers and referencing "typical patterns" rather than specific classes discovered earlier
knowledge.sr: Degradacija konteksta u dugim sesijama: modeli počinju da daju nedosledne odgovore i pominju „tipične obrasce" umesto konkretnih klasa otkrivenih ranije

knowledge.en: The role of scratchpad files for persisting key findings across context boundaries
knowledge.sr: Uloga scratchpad fajlova za čuvanje ključnih nalaza preko granica konteksta

knowledge.en: Subagent delegation for isolating verbose exploration output while the main agent coordinates high-level understanding
knowledge.sr: Delegiranje subagentima za izolaciju opširnog izlaza istraživanja dok glavni agent koordiniše razumevanje na visokom nivou

knowledge.en: Structured state persistence for crash recovery: each agent exports state to a known location, and the coordinator loads a manifest on resume
knowledge.sr: Strukturisano čuvanje stanja za oporavak od pada: svaki agent izvozi stanje na poznatu lokaciju, a koordinator pri nastavku učitava manifest

skills.en: Spawning subagents to investigate specific questions (e.g., "find all test files," "trace refund flow dependencies") while the main agent preserves high-level coordination
skills.sr: Pokretanje subagenata za konkretna pitanja (npr. „nađi sve test fajlove", „prati zavisnosti toka povraćaja") dok glavni agent čuva koordinaciju na visokom nivou

skills.en: Having agents maintain scratchpad files recording key findings, referencing them for subsequent questions to counteract context degradation
skills.sr: Da agenti vode scratchpad fajlove sa ključnim nalazima i pozivaju se na njih za naredna pitanja radi suzbijanja degradacije konteksta

skills.en: Summarizing key findings from one exploration phase before spawning sub-agents for the next phase, injecting summaries into initial context
skills.sr: Sažimanje ključnih nalaza jedne faze istraživanja pre pokretanja subagenata za sledeću fazu, uz ubacivanje sažetaka u početni kontekst

skills.en: Designing crash recovery using structured agent state exports (manifests) that the coordinator loads on resume and injects into agent prompts
skills.sr: Dizajn oporavka od pada preko strukturisanih izvoza stanja agenta (manifesti) koje koordinator učitava pri nastavku i ubacuje u promptove agenata

skills.en: Using /compact to reduce context usage during extended exploration sessions when context fills with verbose discovery output
skills.sr: Korišćenje /compact za smanjenje potrošnje konteksta u dugim sesijama istraživanja kada se kontekst napuni opširnim izlazom

title.en: Design human review workflows and confidence calibration
title.sr: Dizajn tokova ljudskog review-a i kalibracije pouzdanosti

knowledge.en: The risk that aggregate accuracy metrics (e.g., 97% overall) may mask poor performance on specific document types or fields
knowledge.sr: Rizik da agregatne metrike tačnosti (npr. 97% ukupno) maskiraju lošu tačnost na određenim tipovima dokumenata ili poljima

knowledge.en: Stratified random sampling for measuring error rates in high-confidence extractions and detecting novel error patterns
knowledge.sr: Stratifikovano nasumično uzorkovanje za merenje stope grešaka u ekstrakcijama visoke pouzdanosti i otkrivanje novih obrazaca grešaka

knowledge.en: Field-level confidence scores calibrated using labeled validation sets for routing review attention
knowledge.sr: Ocene pouzdanosti na nivou polja, kalibrisane labelovanim validacionim setovima, za usmeravanje pažnje review-a

knowledge.en: The importance of validating accuracy by document type and field segment before automating high-confidence extractions
knowledge.sr: Važnost validacije tačnosti po tipu dokumenta i segmentu polja pre automatizacije ekstrakcija visoke pouzdanosti

skills.en: Implementing stratified random sampling of high-confidence extractions for ongoing error rate measurement and novel pattern detection
skills.sr: Implementacija stratifikovanog uzorkovanja ekstrakcija visoke pouzdanosti za stalno merenje stope grešaka i otkrivanje novih obrazaca

skills.en: Analyzing accuracy by document type and field to verify consistent performance across all segments before reducing human review
skills.sr: Analiza tačnosti po tipu dokumenta i polju radi provere dosledne tačnosti na svim segmentima pre smanjenja ljudskog review-a

skills.en: Having models output field-level confidence scores, then calibrating review thresholds using labeled validation sets
skills.sr: Da modeli daju ocene pouzdanosti na nivou polja, pa kalibracija pragova review-a labelovanim validacionim setovima

skills.en: Routing extractions with low model confidence or ambiguous/contradictory source documents to human review, prioritizing limited reviewer capacity
skills.sr: Rutiranje ekstrakcija sa niskom pouzdanošću ili dvosmislenim/protivrečnim izvorima ka ljudskom review-u, uz prioritizaciju ograničenog kapaciteta recenzenata

title.en: Preserve information provenance and handle uncertainty in multi-source synthesis
title.sr: Očuvanje provenijencije informacija i obrada nesigurnosti u sintezi iz više izvora

knowledge.en: How source attribution is lost during summarization steps when findings are compressed without preserving claim-source mappings
knowledge.sr: Kako se atribucija izvora gubi tokom sažimanja kada se nalazi kompresuju bez očuvanja mapiranja tvrdnja–izvor

knowledge.en: The importance of structured claim-source mappings that the synthesis agent must preserve and merge when combining findings
knowledge.sr: Važnost strukturisanih mapiranja tvrdnja–izvor koje agent za sintezu mora očuvati i spojiti pri kombinovanju nalaza

knowledge.en: How to handle conflicting statistics from credible sources: annotating conflicts with source attribution rather than arbitrarily selecting one value
knowledge.sr: Kako obraditi protivrečne statistike iz pouzdanih izvora: anotiranje sukoba sa atribucijom izvora umesto proizvoljnog biranja jedne vrednosti

knowledge.en: Temporal data: requiring publication/collection dates in structured outputs to prevent temporal differences from being misinterpreted as contradictions
knowledge.sr: Vremenski podaci: zahtevanje datuma objave/prikupljanja u strukturisanim izlazima da se vremenske razlike ne protumače kao protivrečnosti

skills.en: Requiring subagents to output structured claim-source mappings (source URLs, document names, relevant excerpts) that downstream agents preserve through synthesis
skills.sr: Zahtevanje da subagenti daju strukturisana mapiranja tvrdnja–izvor (URL izvora, imena dokumenata, relevantni isečci) koje downstream agenti čuvaju kroz sintezu

skills.en: Structuring reports with explicit sections distinguishing well-established findings from contested ones, preserving original source characterizations and methodological context
skills.sr: Strukturiranje izveštaja sa eksplicitnim sekcijama koje razdvajaju dobro utvrđene nalaze od spornih, uz očuvanje originalnih karakterizacija izvora i metodološkog konteksta

skills.en: Completing document analysis with conflicting values included and explicitly annotated, letting the coordinator decide how to reconcile before passing to synthesis
skills.sr: Završavanje analize dokumenta sa uključenim i eksplicitno anotiranim protivrečnim vrednostima, prepuštajući koordinatoru da odluči kako da ih pomiri pre prosleđivanja sintezi

skills.en: Requiring subagents to include publication or data collection dates in structured outputs to enable correct temporal interpretation
skills.sr: Zahtevanje da subagenti uključe datume objave ili prikupljanja u strukturisane izlaze radi ispravnog vremenskog tumačenja

skills.en: Rendering different content types appropriately in synthesis outputs—financial data as tables, news as prose, technical findings as structured lists—rather than converting everything to a uniform format
skills.sr: Primereno prikazivanje različitih tipova sadržaja u sintezi — finansijski podaci kao tabele, vesti kao proza, tehnički nalazi kao strukturisane liste — umesto svođenja svega na jedinstven format

title.en: Sample Questions
title.sr: Primeri pitanja

intro.en: The following sample questions illustrate the format and difficulty level of the exam. These are drawn from the practice test and include explanations to aid learning.
intro.sr: Sledeća primer-pitanja ilustruju format i nivo težine ispita. Preuzeta su iz probnog testa i sadrže objašnjenja radi učenja.

question.en: Production data shows that in 12% of cases, your agent skips get_customer entirely and calls lookup_order using only the customer's stated name, occasionally leading to misidentified accounts and incorrect refunds. What change would most effectively address this reliability issue?
question.sr: Produkcioni podaci pokazuju da u 12% slučajeva agent potpuno preskoči get_customer i pozove lookup_order koristeći samo ime koje je korisnik naveo, što povremeno vodi pogrešno identifikovanim nalozima i netačnim povraćajima. Koja izmena bi najefikasnije rešila ovaj problem pouzdanosti?

A.en: Add a programmatic prerequisite that blocks lookup_order and process_refund calls until get_customer has returned a verified customer ID.
A.sr: Dodaj programski preduslov koji blokira pozive lookup_order i process_refund dok get_customer ne vrati verifikovan ID korisnika.

B.en: Enhance the system prompt to state that customer verification via get_customer is mandatory before any order operations.
B.sr: Dopuni sistemski prompt da je verifikacija korisnika preko get_customer obavezna pre bilo koje operacije sa porudžbinom.

C.en: Add few-shot examples showing the agent always calling get_customer first, even when customers volunteer order details.
C.sr: Dodaj few-shot primere koji pokazuju da agent uvek prvo poziva get_customer, čak i kad korisnik sam navede detalje porudžbine.

D.en: Implement a routing classifier that analyzes each request and enables only the subset of tools appropriate for that request type.
D.sr: Implementiraj routing klasifikator koji analizira svaki zahtev i omogućava samo podskup alata primeren tom tipu zahteva.

explanation.en: When a specific tool sequence is required for critical business logic (like verifying customer identity before processing refunds), programmatic enforcement provides deterministic guarantees that prompt-based approaches cannot. Options B and C rely on probabilistic LLM compliance, which is insufficient when errors have financial consequences. Option D addresses tool availability rather than tool ordering, which is not the actual problem.
explanation.sr: Kada je za kritičnu poslovnu logiku potreban tačan redosled alata (kao verifikacija identiteta pre povraćaja), programsko sprovođenje daje determinističku garanciju koju prompt ne može. B i C se oslanjaju na probabilističku poslušnost modela, što je nedovoljno kad greške imaju finansijske posledice. D rešava dostupnost alata, a ne redosled — što nije stvarni problem.

question.en: Production logs show the agent frequently calls get_customer when users ask about orders (e.g., "check my order #12345"), instead of calling lookup_order. Both tools have minimal descriptions ("Retrieves customer information" / "Retrieves order details") and accept similar identifier formats. What's the most effective first step to improve tool selection reliability?
question.sr: Logovi pokazuju da agent često poziva get_customer kada korisnici pitaju o porudžbinama (npr. „proveri moju porudžbinu #12345"), umesto lookup_order. Oba alata imaju minimalne opise („Retrieves customer information" / „Retrieves order details") i prihvataju slične formate identifikatora. Koji je najefikasniji prvi korak za poboljšanje pouzdanosti izbora alata?

A.en: Add few-shot examples to the system prompt demonstrating correct tool selection patterns, with 5-8 examples showing order-related queries routing to lookup_order.
A.sr: Dodaj few-shot primere u sistemski prompt koji demonstriraju ispravan izbor alata, sa 5–8 primera gde upiti o porudžbinama idu na lookup_order.

B.en: Expand each tool's description to include input formats it handles, example queries, edge cases, and boundaries explaining when to use it versus similar tools.
B.sr: Proširi opis svakog alata da uključi formate ulaza koje obrađuje, primere upita, edge case-ove i granice koje objašnjavaju kada ga koristiti naspram sličnih alata.

C.en: Implement a routing layer that parses user input before each turn and pre-selects the appropriate tool based on detected keywords and identifier patterns.
C.sr: Implementiraj routing sloj koji parsira korisnički unos pre svakog poteza i unapred bira odgovarajući alat na osnovu detektovanih ključnih reči i obrazaca identifikatora.

D.en: Consolidate both tools into a single lookup_entity tool that accepts any identifier and internally determines which backend to query.
D.sr: Spoji oba alata u jedan lookup_entity koji prihvata bilo koji identifikator i interno odlučuje koji backend da upita.

explanation.en: Tool descriptions are the primary mechanism LLMs use for tool selection. When descriptions are minimal, models lack the context to differentiate between similar tools. Option B directly addresses this root cause with a low-effort, high-leverage fix. Few-shot examples (A) add token overhead without fixing the underlying issue. A routing layer (C) is over-engineered and bypasses the LLM's natural language understanding. Consolidating tools (D) is a valid architectural choice but requires more effort than a "first step" warrants when the immediate problem is inadequate descriptions.
explanation.sr: Opisi alata su primarni mehanizam za izbor alata. Kad su minimalni, modelu nedostaje kontekst da razlikuje slične alate. B direktno rešava taj koren uz mali trud i veliki efekat. Few-shot (A) dodaje potrošnju tokena bez rešavanja uzroka. Routing sloj (C) je preterano inženjerstvo i zaobilazi razumevanje prirodnog jezika. Spajanje alata (D) je validno ali traži više truda nego što „prvi korak" nalaže kad je neposredni problem loš opis.

question.en: Your agent achieves 55% first-contact resolution, well below the 80% target. Logs show it escalates straightforward cases (standard damage replacements with photo evidence) while attempting to autonomously handle complex situations requiring policy exceptions. What's the most effective way to improve escalation calibration?
question.sr: Agent postiže 55% rešavanja u prvom kontaktu, znatno ispod cilja od 80%. Logovi pokazuju da eskalira jednostavne slučajeve (standardne zamene oštećene robe sa foto-dokazom) dok pokušava sam da reši složene situacije koje traže izuzetke od politike. Koji je najefikasniji način da se poboljša kalibracija eskalacije?

A.en: Add explicit escalation criteria to your system prompt with few-shot examples demonstrating when to escalate versus resolve autonomously.
A.sr: Dodaj eksplicitne kriterijume eskalacije u sistemski prompt sa few-shot primerima koji pokazuju kada eskalirati a kada rešiti samostalno.

B.en: Have the agent self-report a confidence score (1-10) before each response and automatically route requests to humans when confidence falls below a threshold.
B.sr: Neka agent pre svakog odgovora sam prijavi ocenu pouzdanosti (1–10) i automatski prosledi čoveku kad pouzdanost padne ispod praga.

C.en: Deploy a separate classifier model trained on historical tickets to predict which requests need escalation before the main agent begins processing.
C.sr: Postavi zaseban klasifikatorski model treniran na istorijskim tiketima da predvidi koji zahtevi traže eskalaciju pre nego što glavni agent počne obradu.

D.en: Implement sentiment analysis to detect customer frustration levels and automatically escalate when negative sentiment exceeds a threshold.
D.sr: Implementiraj analizu sentimenta za detekciju nivoa frustracije korisnika i automatski eskaliraj kad negativan sentiment pređe prag.

explanation.en: Adding explicit escalation criteria with few-shot examples directly addresses the root cause: unclear decision boundaries. This is the proportionate first response before adding infrastructure. Option B fails because LLM self-reported confidence is poorly calibrated—the agent is already incorrectly confident on hard cases. Option C is over-engineered, requiring labeled data and ML infrastructure when prompt optimization hasn't been tried. Option D solves a different problem entirely; sentiment doesn't correlate with case complexity, which is the actual issue.
explanation.sr: Eksplicitni kriterijumi eskalacije sa few-shot primerima direktno rešavaju koren: nejasne granice odlučivanja. To je srazmeran prvi odgovor pre dodavanja infrastrukture. B pada jer je samoprijavljena pouzdanost loše kalibrisana — agent je već pogrešno siguran na teškim slučajevima. C je preterano (traži labelovane podatke i ML infrastrukturu pre nego što je proba optimizacije prompta). D rešava sasvim drugi problem; sentiment ne korelira sa složenošću slučaja, što je stvarni problem.

question.en: You want to create a custom /review slash command that runs your team's standard code review checklist. This command should be available to every developer when they clone or pull the repository. Where should you create this command file?
question.sr: Želiš da napraviš custom /review slash komandu koja pokreće standardni checklist code review-a tvog tima. Komanda treba da bude dostupna svakom developeru kad klonira ili pull-uje repozitorijum. Gde treba da napraviš fajl komande?

A.en: In the .claude/commands/ directory in the project repository

B.en: In ~/.claude/commands/ in each developer's home directory
B.sr: U ~/.claude/commands/ u home direktorijumu svakog developera

C.en: In the CLAUDE.md file at the project root
C.sr: U CLAUDE.md fajlu u korenu projekta

D.en: In a .claude/config.json file with a commands array

explanation.en: Project-scoped custom slash commands should be stored in the .claude/commands/ directory within the repository. These commands are version-controlled and automatically available to all developers when they clone or pull the repo. Option B (~/.claude/commands/) is for personal commands that aren't shared via version control. Option C (CLAUDE.md) is for project instructions and context, not command definitions. Option D describes a configuration mechanism that doesn't exist in Claude Code.
explanation.sr: Slash komande na nivou projekta idu u .claude/commands/ unutar repozitorijuma. One su pod version control-om i automatski dostupne svima pri kloniranju/pull-u. B (~/.claude/commands/) je za lične komande koje se ne dele. C (CLAUDE.md) je za instrukcije i kontekst projekta, ne za definicije komandi. D opisuje konfiguracioni mehanizam koji ne postoji u Claude Code-u.

question.en: You've been assigned to restructure the team's monolithic application into microservices. This will involve changes across dozens of files and requires decisions about service boundaries and module dependencies. Which approach should you take?
question.sr: Dobio si zadatak da restrukturiraš monolitnu aplikaciju tima u mikroservise. To uključuje izmene u desetinama fajlova i traži odluke o granicama servisa i zavisnostima modula. Koji pristup treba da izabereš?

A.en: Enter plan mode to explore the codebase, understand dependencies, and design an implementation approach before making changes.
A.sr: Uđi u plan mod da istražiš bazu, razumeš zavisnosti i dizajniraš pristup implementaciji pre izmena.

B.en: Start with direct execution and make changes incrementally, letting the implementation reveal the natural service boundaries.
B.sr: Kreni direktnim izvršavanjem i menjaj inkrementalno, puštajući da implementacija otkrije prirodne granice servisa.

C.en: Use direct execution with comprehensive upfront instructions detailing exactly how each service should be structured.
C.sr: Koristi direktno izvršavanje sa sveobuhvatnim instrukcijama unapred koje tačno opisuju kako svaki servis treba da bude struktuiran.

D.en: Begin in direct execution mode and only switch to plan mode if you encounter unexpected complexity during implementation.
D.sr: Počni u direktnom izvršavanju i pređi u plan mod samo ako naiđeš na neočekivanu složenost tokom implementacije.

explanation.en: Plan mode is designed for complex tasks involving large-scale changes, multiple valid approaches, and architectural decisions—exactly what monolith-to-microservices restructuring requires. It enables safe codebase exploration and design before committing to changes. Option B risks costly rework when dependencies are discovered late. Option C assumes you already know the right structure without exploring the code. Option D ignores that the complexity is already stated in the requirements, not something that might emerge later.
explanation.sr: Plan mod je za složene zadatke sa velikim izmenama, više validnih pristupa i arhitektonskim odlukama — baš ono što traži prelazak monolit→mikroservisi. Omogućava bezbedno istraživanje i dizajn pre izmena. B rizikuje skupu doradu kad se zavisnosti otkriju kasno. C pretpostavlja da već znaš pravu strukturu bez istraživanja koda. D ignoriše da je složenost već navedena u zahtevima, a ne nešto što tek može da iskrsne.

question.en: Your codebase has distinct areas with different coding conventions: React components use functional style with hooks, API handlers use async/await with specific error handling, and database models follow a repository pattern. Test files are spread throughout the codebase alongside the code they test (e.g., Button.test.tsx next to Button.tsx), and you want all tests to follow the same conventions regardless of location. What's the most maintainable way to ensure Claude automatically applies the correct conventions when generating code?
question.sr: Tvoja baza ima različite oblasti sa različitim konvencijama: React komponente koriste funkcionalni stil sa hook-ovima, API handler-i async/await sa specifičnim error handling-om, a database modeli repository obrazac. Test fajlovi su razbacani po bazi uz kod koji testiraju (npr. Button.test.tsx pored Button.tsx), i želiš da svi testovi prate iste konvencije bez obzira na lokaciju. Koji je najodrživiji način da Claude automatski primeni ispravne konvencije pri generisanju koda?

A.en: Create rule files in .claude/rules/ with YAML frontmatter specifying glob patterns to conditionally apply conventions based on file paths
A.sr: Napravi fajlove pravila u .claude/rules/ sa YAML frontmatter-om koji zadaje glob obrasce da se konvencije uslovno primene prema putanjama fajlova

B.en: Consolidate all conventions in the root CLAUDE.md file under headers for each area, relying on Claude to infer which section applies
B.sr: Objedini sve konvencije u root CLAUDE.md pod naslovima za svaku oblast, oslanjajući se na to da Claude zaključi koja sekcija važi

C.en: Create skills in .claude/skills/ for each code type that include the relevant conventions in their SKILL.md files
C.sr: Napravi skills u .claude/skills/ za svaki tip koda koji u svojim SKILL.md fajlovima sadrže relevantne konvencije

D.en: Place a separate CLAUDE.md file in each subdirectory containing that area's specific conventions
D.sr: Stavi zaseban CLAUDE.md u svaki poddirektorijum sa specifičnim konvencijama te oblasti

explanation.en: Option A is correct because .claude/rules/ with glob patterns (e.g., **/*.test.tsx) allows conventions to be automatically applied based on file paths regardless of directory location—essential for test files spread throughout the codebase. Option B relies on inference rather than explicit matching, making it unreliable. Option C requires manual skill invocation or relies on Claude choosing to load them, contradicting the need for deterministic "automatic" application based on file paths. Option D can't easily handle files spread across many directories since CLAUDE.md files are directory-bound.
explanation.sr: A je tačno jer .claude/rules/ sa glob obrascima (npr. **/*.test.tsx) primenjuje konvencije automatski prema putanji bez obzira na direktorijum — ključno za test fajlove razbacane po bazi. B se oslanja na zaključivanje umesto na eksplicitno poklapanje, pa je nepouzdano. C traži ručni poziv skill-a ili da ih Claude sam učita, što protivreči potrebi za determinističkom „automatskom" primenom po putanji. D teško pokriva fajlove razbacane po mnogo direktorijuma jer su CLAUDE.md fajlovi vezani za direktorijum.

question.en: After running the system on the topic "impact of AI on creative industries," you observe that each subagent completes successfully: the web search agent finds relevant articles, the document analysis agent summarizes papers correctly, and the synthesis agent produces coherent output. However, the final reports cover only visual arts, completely missing music, writing, and film production. When you examine the coordinator's logs, you see it decomposed the topic into three subtasks: "AI in digital art creation," "AI in graphic design," and "AI in photography." What is the most likely root cause?
question.sr: Nakon pokretanja sistema na temi „uticaj AI na kreativne industrije", primećuješ da svaki subagent uspešno završava: web pretraga nalazi relevantne članke, analiza dokumenata ispravno sažima radove, a sinteza daje koherentan izlaz. Ipak, finalni izveštaji pokrivaju samo vizuelne umetnosti, potpuno izostavljajući muziku, pisanje i film. U logovima koordinatora vidiš da je temu razložio na tri podzadatka: „AI u digitalnoj umetnosti", „AI u grafičkom dizajnu" i „AI u fotografiji". Koji je najverovatniji uzrok?

A.en: The synthesis agent lacks instructions for identifying coverage gaps in the findings it receives from other agents.
A.sr: Agentu za sintezu nedostaju instrukcije za prepoznavanje rupa u pokrivenosti u nalazima koje dobija od drugih agenata.

B.en: The coordinator agent's task decomposition is too narrow, resulting in subagent assignments that don't cover all relevant domains of the topic.
B.sr: Dekompozicija zadatka kod koordinatora je preuska, pa zadaci subagentima ne pokrivaju sve relevantne domene teme.

C.en: The web search agent's queries are not comprehensive enough and need to be expanded to cover more creative industry sectors.
C.sr: Upiti agenta za web pretragu nisu dovoljno sveobuhvatni i treba ih proširiti na više sektora kreativnih industrija.

D.en: The document analysis agent is filtering out sources related to non-visual creative industries due to overly restrictive relevance criteria.
D.sr: Agent za analizu dokumenata filtrira izvore vezane za nevizuelne kreativne industrije zbog previše restriktivnih kriterijuma relevantnosti.

explanation.en: The coordinator's logs reveal the root cause directly: it decomposed "creative industries" into only visual arts subtasks (digital art, graphic design, photography), completely omitting music, writing, and film. The subagents executed their assigned tasks correctly—the problem is what they were assigned. Options A, C, and D incorrectly blame downstream agents that are working correctly within their assigned scope.
explanation.sr: Logovi koordinatora direktno otkrivaju uzrok: „kreativne industrije" je razložio samo na vizuelne (digitalna umetnost, grafički dizajn, fotografija), izostavivši muziku, pisanje i film. Subagenti su tačno izvršili dodeljeno — problem je u tome ŠTA im je dodeljeno. A, C i D pogrešno krive downstream agente koji rade ispravno u okviru dodeljenog opsega.

question.en: The web search subagent times out while researching a complex topic. You need to design how this failure information flows back to the coordinator agent. Which error propagation approach best enables intelligent recovery?
question.sr: Subagent za web pretragu doživi timeout tokom istraživanja složene teme. Treba da dizajniraš kako ta informacija o otkazu stiže nazad do koordinatora. Koji pristup propagaciji greške najbolje omogućava inteligentan oporavak?

A.en: Return structured error context to the coordinator including the failure type, the attempted query, any partial results, and potential alternative approaches.
A.sr: Vrati koordinatoru strukturisan kontekst greške: tip otkaza, pokušani upit, eventualne parcijalne rezultate i moguće alternativne pristupe.

B.en: Implement automatic retry logic with exponential backoff within the subagent, returning a generic "search unavailable" status only after all retries are exhausted.
B.sr: Implementiraj automatski retry sa exponential backoff-om unutar subagenta, vraćajući generički status „search unavailable" tek kad se svi pokušaji iscrpe.

C.en: Catch the timeout within the subagent and return an empty result set marked as successful.
C.sr: Uhvati timeout u subagentu i vrati prazan skup rezultata označen kao uspešan.

D.en: Propagate the timeout exception directly to a top-level handler that terminates the entire research workflow.
D.sr: Prosledi timeout izuzetak direktno top-level handler-u koji obara ceo istraživački tok.

explanation.en: Structured error context gives the coordinator the information it needs to make intelligent recovery decisions—whether to retry with a modified query, try an alternative approach, or proceed with partial results. Option B's generic status hides valuable context from the coordinator, preventing informed decisions. Option C suppresses the error by marking failure as success, which prevents any recovery and risks incomplete research outputs. Option D terminates the entire workflow unnecessarily when recovery strategies could succeed.
explanation.sr: Strukturisan kontekst greške daje koordinatoru informacije za inteligentnu odluku o oporavku — da li da ponovi sa izmenjenim upitom, proba alternativu ili nastavi sa parcijalnim rezultatima. Generički status (B) sakriva vredan kontekst i sprečava informisanu odluku. C guši grešku označavajući otkaz kao uspeh, što sprečava oporavak i rizikuje nepotpune rezultate. D nepotrebno obara ceo tok kad bi oporavak mogao da uspe.

question.en: During testing, you observe that the synthesis agent frequently needs to verify specific claims while combining findings. Currently, when verification is needed, the synthesis agent returns control to the coordinator, which invokes the web search agent, then re-invokes synthesis with results. This adds 2-3 round trips per task and increases latency by 40%. Your evaluation shows that 85% of these verifications are simple fact-checks (dates, names, statistics) while 15% require deeper investigation. What's the most effective approach to reduce overhead while maintaining system reliability?
question.sr: Tokom testiranja primećuješ da agent za sintezu često mora da proveri konkretne tvrdnje dok kombinuje nalaze. Trenutno, kad treba provera, sinteza vraća kontrolu koordinatoru, koji poziva web pretragu, pa ponovo poziva sintezu sa rezultatima. To dodaje 2–3 obilaska po zadatku i povećava latenciju za 40%. Evaluacija pokazuje da je 85% ovih provera prosto proveravanje činjenica (datumi, imena, statistike), a 15% traži dublju istragu. Koji je najefikasniji pristup da se smanji overhead uz očuvanje pouzdanosti?

A.en: Give the synthesis agent a scoped verify_fact tool for simple lookups, while complex verifications continue delegating to the web search agent through the coordinator.
A.sr: Daj agentu za sintezu ograničen verify_fact alat za jednostavne provere, dok složene provere i dalje idu na web pretragu kroz koordinatora.

B.en: Have the synthesis agent accumulate all verification needs and return them as a batch to the coordinator at the end of its pass, which then sends them all to the web search agent at once.
B.sr: Neka agent za sintezu sakupi sve potrebe za proverom i vrati ih koordinatoru kao batch na kraju prolaza, koji ih sve odjednom šalje web pretraži.

C.en: Give the synthesis agent access to all web search tools so it can handle any verification need directly without round-trips through the coordinator.
C.sr: Daj agentu za sintezu pristup svim alatima web pretrage da svaku proveru reši direktno bez obilazaka kroz koordinatora.

D.en: Have the web search agent proactively cache extra context around each source during initial research, anticipating what the synthesis agent might need to verify.
D.sr: Neka web pretraga proaktivno kešira dodatni kontekst oko svakog izvora tokom početnog istraživanja, predviđajući šta bi sinteza mogla da proverava.

explanation.en: Option A applies the principle of least privilege by giving the synthesis agent only what it needs for the 85% common case (simple fact verification) while preserving the existing coordination pattern for complex cases. Option B's batching approach creates blocking dependencies since synthesis steps may depend on earlier verified facts. Option C over-provisions the synthesis agent, violating separation of concerns. Option D relies on speculative caching that cannot reliably predict what the synthesis agent will need to verify.
explanation.sr: A primenjuje princip najmanjih privilegija — daje sintezi tačno ono što treba za 85% čestih slučajeva (prosta provera), uz očuvanje postojećeg obrasca koordinacije za složene. Batch (B) stvara blokirajuće zavisnosti jer koraci sinteze mogu zavisiti od ranije proverenih činjenica. C preopremu agenta za sintezu, kršeći separation of concerns. D se oslanja na spekulativni keš koji ne može pouzdano da predvidi šta će sinteza proveravati.

question.en: Your pipeline script runs claude "Analyze this pull request for security issues" but the job hangs indefinitely. Logs indicate Claude Code is waiting for interactive input. What's the correct approach to run Claude Code in an automated pipeline?
question.sr: Tvoja pipeline skripta pokreće claude "Analyze this pull request for security issues" ali se posao zaglavi unedogled. Logovi pokazuju da Claude Code čeka interaktivni unos. Koji je ispravan pristup za pokretanje Claude Code-a u automatizovanom pipeline-u?

A.en: Add the -p flag: claude -p "Analyze this pull request for security issues"
A.sr: Dodaj -p flag: claude -p "Analyze this pull request for security issues"

B.en: Set the environment variable CLAUDE_HEADLESS=true before running the command
B.sr: Postavi environment varijablu CLAUDE_HEADLESS=true pre pokretanja komande

C.en: Redirect stdin from /dev/null: claude "Analyze this pull request for security issues" < /dev/null
C.sr: Preusmeri stdin sa /dev/null: claude "Analyze this pull request for security issues" < /dev/null

D.en: Add the --batch flag: claude --batch "Analyze this pull request for security issues"
D.sr: Dodaj --batch flag: claude --batch "Analyze this pull request for security issues"

explanation.en: The -p (or --print) flag is the documented way to run Claude Code in non-interactive mode. It processes the prompt, outputs the result to stdout, and exits without waiting for user input—exactly what CI/CD pipelines require. The other options reference non-existent features (CLAUDE_HEADLESS environment variable, --batch flag) or use Unix workarounds that don't properly address Claude Code's command syntax.
explanation.sr: Flag -p (ili --print) je dokumentovani način za neinteraktivni režim. Obradi prompt, ispiše rezultat na stdout i izađe bez čekanja na unos — baš ono što CI/CD traži. Ostale opcije pominju nepostojeće funkcije (CLAUDE_HEADLESS varijabla, --batch flag) ili koriste Unix zaobilaznice koje ne rešavaju sintaksu komande Claude Code-a.

question.en: Your team wants to reduce API costs for automated analysis. Currently, real-time Claude calls power two workflows: (1) a blocking pre-merge check that must complete before developers can merge, and (2) a technical debt report generated overnight for review the next morning. Your manager proposes switching both to the Message Batches API for its 50% cost savings. How should you evaluate this proposal?
question.sr: Tim želi da smanji API troškove za automatsku analizu. Trenutno real-time Claude pozivi pokreću dva toka: (1) blokirajuću pre-merge proveru koja mora da se završi pre merge-a, i (2) izveštaj o tehničkom dugu generisan preko noći za pregled sledećeg jutra. Menadžer predlaže da se oba prebace na Message Batches API zbog 50% uštede. Kako treba da proceniš taj predlog?

A.en: Use batch processing for the technical debt reports only; keep real-time calls for pre-merge checks.
A.sr: Koristi batch obradu samo za izveštaje o tehničkom dugu; zadrži real-time pozive za pre-merge provere.

B.en: Switch both workflows to batch processing with status polling to check for completion.
B.sr: Prebaci oba toka na batch obradu uz polling statusa radi provere završetka.

C.en: Keep real-time calls for both workflows to avoid batch result ordering issues.
C.sr: Zadrži real-time pozive za oba toka da izbegneš probleme sa redosledom batch rezultata.

D.en: Switch both to batch processing with a timeout fallback to real-time if batches take too long.
D.sr: Prebaci oba na batch uz timeout fallback na real-time ako batch-evi predugo traju.

explanation.en: The Message Batches API offers 50% cost savings but has processing times up to 24 hours with no guaranteed latency SLA. This makes it unsuitable for blocking pre-merge checks where developers wait for results, but ideal for overnight batch jobs like technical debt reports. Option B is wrong because relying on "often faster" completion isn't acceptable for blocking workflows. Option C reflects a misconception—batch results can be correlated using custom_id fields. Option D adds unnecessary complexity when the simpler solution is matching each API to its appropriate use case.
explanation.sr: Message Batches API daje 50% uštede ali ima obradu do 24h bez garantovanog SLA za latenciju. Zato nije pogodan za blokirajuće pre-merge provere gde developeri čekaju rezultat, ali je idealan za noćne poslove kao izveštaj o tehničkom dugu. B je pogrešno jer oslanjanje na „često brže" nije prihvatljivo za blokirajuće tokove. C je zabluda — batch rezultati se uparuju preko custom_id polja. D dodaje nepotrebnu složenost kad je jednostavnije uskladiti svaki API sa svojom namenom.

question.en: A pull request modifies 14 files across the stock tracking module. Your single-pass review analyzing all files together produces inconsistent results: detailed feedback for some files but superficial comments for others, obvious bugs missed, and contradictory feedback—flagging a pattern as problematic in one file while approving identical code elsewhere in the same PR. How should you restructure the review?
question.sr: Pull request menja 14 fajlova u modulu za praćenje zaliha. Tvoj review u jednom prolazu (svi fajlovi zajedno) daje nedosledne rezultate: detaljan feedback za neke fajlove a površan za druge, propuštene očigledne bagove i protivrečan feedback — označavanje obrasca kao problematičnog u jednom fajlu uz odobravanje identičnog koda na drugom mestu u istom PR-u. Kako treba da restrukturiraš review?

A.en: Split into focused passes: analyze each file individually for local issues, then run a separate integration-focused pass examining cross-file data flow.
A.sr: Podeli na fokusirane prolaze: analiziraj svaki fajl pojedinačno za lokalne probleme, pa pokreni zaseban integracioni prolaz koji ispituje protok podataka između fajlova.

B.en: Require developers to split large PRs into smaller submissions of 3-4 files before the automated review runs.
B.sr: Zahtevaj da developeri podele velike PR-ove na manje od 3–4 fajla pre automatskog review-a.

C.en: Switch to a higher-tier model with a larger context window to give all 14 files adequate attention in one pass.
C.sr: Pređi na jači model sa većim context window-om da svih 14 fajlova dobije adekvatnu pažnju u jednom prolazu.

D.en: Run three independent review passes on the full PR and only flag issues that appear in at least two of the three runs.
D.sr: Pokreni tri nezavisna prolaza review-a nad celim PR-om i označi samo probleme koji se pojave u bar dva od tri prolaza.

explanation.en: Splitting reviews into focused passes directly addresses the root cause: attention dilution when processing many files at once. File-by-file analysis ensures consistent depth, while a separate integration pass catches cross-file issues. Option B shifts burden to developers without improving the system. Option C misunderstands that larger context windows don't solve attention quality issues. Option D would actually suppress detection of real bugs by requiring consensus on issues that may only be caught intermittently.
explanation.sr: Deljenje review-a na fokusirane prolaze direktno rešava koren: attention dilution pri obradi mnogo fajlova odjednom. Analiza fajl-po-fajl daje doslednu dubinu, a zaseban integracioni prolaz hvata probleme između fajlova. B prebacuje teret na developere bez popravke sistema. C pogrešno veruje da veći context window rešava kvalitet pažnje. D bi zapravo ugušio detekciju stvarnih bagova tražeći konsenzus o problemima koji se hvataju samo povremeno.

title.en: Preparation Exercises
title.sr: Vežbe za pripremu

intro.en: Complete these hands-on exercises to build practical familiarity with the topics covered on the exam. Each exercise is designed to reinforce knowledge across one or more exam domains.
intro.sr: Uradi ove praktične vežbe da izgradiš praktičnu prisnost sa temama ispita. Svaka vežba učvršćuje znanje iz jednog ili više domena.

title.en: Build a Multi-Tool Agent with Escalation Logic
title.sr: Napravi multi-tool agenta sa logikom eskalacije

objective.en: Practice designing an agentic loop with tool integration, structured error handling, and escalation patterns.
objective.sr: Vežbaj dizajn agentske petlje sa integracijom alata, strukturisanim error handling-om i obrascima eskalacije.

steps.en: Define 3-4 MCP tools with detailed descriptions that clearly differentiate each tool's purpose, expected inputs, and boundary conditions. Include at least two tools with similar functionality that require careful description to avoid selection confusion.
steps.sr: Definiši 3–4 MCP alata sa detaljnim opisima koji jasno razlikuju svrhu, očekivane ulaze i granične uslove. Uključi bar dva alata slične funkcije koji traže pažljiv opis da se izbegne zabuna pri izboru.

steps.en: Implement an agentic loop that checks stop_reason to determine whether to continue tool execution or present the final response. Handle both "tool_use" and "end_turn" stop reasons correctly.
steps.sr: Implementiraj agentsku petlju koja proverava stop_reason da odluči da li nastavlja izvršavanje alata ili daje finalni odgovor. Ispravno obradi i "tool_use" i "end_turn".

steps.en: Add structured error responses to your tools: include errorCategory (transient/validation/permission), isRetryable boolean, and human-readable descriptions. Test that the agent handles each error type appropriately (retrying transient errors, explaining business errors to the user).
steps.sr: Dodaj strukturisane greške alatima: errorCategory (transient/validation/permission), isRetryable boolean i ljudski-čitljiv opis. Testiraj da agent primereno obrađuje svaki tip (ponavlja tranzijentne, objašnjava poslovne korisniku).

steps.en: Implement a programmatic hook that intercepts tool calls to enforce a business rule (e.g., blocking operations above a threshold amount), redirecting to an escalation workflow when triggered.
steps.sr: Implementiraj programski hook koji presreće pozive alata radi sprovođenja poslovnog pravila (npr. blokiranje operacija iznad praga), uz preusmeravanje na tok eskalacije kad se okine.

steps.en: Test with multi-concern messages (e.g., requests involving multiple issues) and verify the agent decomposes the request, handles each concern, and synthesizes a unified response.
steps.sr: Testiraj sa višeproblemskim porukama (npr. zahtevi sa više stavki) i proveri da agent razloži zahtev, obradi svaku stavku i sintetiše jedinstven odgovor.

domains_reinforced.en: Domain 1 (Agentic Architecture & Orchestration), Domain 2 (Tool Design & MCP Integration), Domain 5 (Context Management & Reliability)
domains_reinforced.sr: Domen 1 (Agentska arhitektura), Domen 2 (Dizajn alata i MCP), Domen 5 (Kontekst i pouzdanost)

title.en: Configure Claude Code for a Team Development Workflow
title.sr: Konfiguriši Claude Code za timski razvojni tok

objective.en: Practice configuring CLAUDE.md hierarchies, custom slash commands, path-specific rules, and MCP server integration for a multi-developer project.
objective.sr: Vežbaj konfiguraciju CLAUDE.md hijerarhija, custom slash komandi, pravila po putanji i integracije MCP servera za projekat sa više developera.

steps.en: Create a project-level CLAUDE.md with universal coding standards and testing conventions. Verify that instructions placed at the project level are consistently applied across all team members.
steps.sr: Napravi CLAUDE.md na nivou projekta sa univerzalnim standardima koda i konvencijama testiranja. Proveri da se instrukcije na nivou projekta dosledno primenjuju kod svih članova tima.

steps.en: Create .claude/rules/ files with YAML frontmatter glob patterns for different code areas (e.g., paths: ["src/api/**/*"] for API conventions, paths: ["**/*.test.*"] for testing conventions). Test that rules load only when editing matching files.
steps.sr: Napravi .claude/rules/ fajlove sa YAML frontmatter glob obrascima za različite oblasti koda (npr. paths: ["src/api/**/*"] za API konvencije, paths: ["**/*.test.*"] za testove). Testiraj da se pravila učitavaju samo pri izmeni odgovarajućih fajlova.

steps.en: Create a project-scoped skill in .claude/skills/ with context: fork and allowed-tools restrictions. Verify the skill runs in isolation without polluting the main conversation context.
steps.sr: Napravi skill na nivou projekta u .claude/skills/ sa context: fork i allowed-tools ograničenjima. Proveri da se skill izvršava izolovano bez zagađivanja glavnog konteksta.

steps.en: Configure an MCP server in .mcp.json with environment variable expansion for credentials. Add a personal experimental MCP server in ~/.claude.json and verify both are available simultaneously.
steps.sr: Konfiguriši MCP server u .mcp.json sa ekspanzijom environment varijabli za kredencijale. Dodaj lični eksperimentalni MCP server u ~/.claude.json i proveri da su oba istovremeno dostupna.

steps.en: Test plan mode versus direct execution on tasks of varying complexity: a single-file bug fix, a multi-file library migration, and a new feature with multiple valid implementation approaches. Observe when plan mode provides value.
steps.sr: Testiraj plan mod naspram direktnog izvršavanja na zadacima različite složenosti: bug fix u jednom fajlu, migracija biblioteke kroz više fajlova i nova funkcija sa više validnih pristupa. Posmatraj kada plan mod donosi vrednost.

domains_reinforced.en: Domain 3 (Claude Code Configuration & Workflows), Domain 2 (Tool Design & MCP Integration)
domains_reinforced.sr: Domen 3 (Konfiguracija Claude Code-a), Domen 2 (Dizajn alata i MCP)

title.en: Build a Structured Data Extraction Pipeline
title.sr: Napravi pipeline za ekstrakciju strukturisanih podataka

objective.en: Practice designing JSON schemas, using tool_use for structured output, implementing validation-retry loops, and designing batch processing strategies.
objective.sr: Vežbaj dizajn JSON šema, korišćenje tool_use za strukturisan izlaz, validation-retry petlje i strategije batch obrade.

steps.en: Define an extraction tool with a JSON schema containing required and optional fields, an enum with an "other" + detail string pattern, and nullable fields for information that may not exist in source documents. Process documents where some fields are absent and verify the model returns null rather than fabricating values.
steps.sr: Definiši alat za ekstrakciju sa JSON šemom koja ima obavezna i opciona polja, enum sa „other" + detail string obrascem i nullable polja za informacije koje možda ne postoje u izvoru. Obradi dokumente gde nedostaju neka polja i proveri da model vrati null umesto da izmišlja vrednosti.

steps.en: Implement a validation-retry loop: when Pydantic or JSON schema validation fails, send a follow-up request including the document, the failed extraction, and the specific validation error. Track which errors are resolvable via retry (format mismatches) versus which are not (information absent from source).
steps.sr: Implementiraj validation-retry petlju: kad Pydantic ili JSON validacija padne, pošalji naredni zahtev sa dokumentom, neuspelom ekstrakcijom i konkretnom greškom. Prati koje su greške rešive ponavljanjem (neslaganje formata) a koje nisu (informacija ne postoji u izvoru).

steps.en: Add few-shot examples demonstrating extraction from documents with varied formats (e.g., inline citations vs bibliographies, narrative descriptions vs structured tables) and verify improved handling of structural variety.
steps.sr: Dodaj few-shot primere koji demonstriraju ekstrakciju iz dokumenata raznolikih formata (npr. inline čitati vs bibliografije, narativni opisi vs strukturisane tabele) i proveri bolju obradu strukturne raznolikosti.

steps.en: Design a batch processing strategy: submit a batch of 100 documents using the Message Batches API, handle failures by custom_id, resubmit failed documents with modifications (e.g., chunking oversized documents), and calculate total processing time relative to SLA constraints.
steps.sr: Dizajniraj strategiju batch obrade: pošalji batch od 100 dokumenata preko Message Batches API-ja, obradi neuspehe po custom_id, ponovo pošalji neuspele uz izmene (npr. seckanje prevelikih dokumenata) i izračunaj ukupno vreme obrade u odnosu na SLA ograničenja.

steps.en: Implement a human review routing strategy: have the model output field-level confidence scores, route low-confidence extractions to human review, and analyze accuracy by document type and field to verify consistent performance.
steps.sr: Implementiraj strategiju rutiranja ka ljudskom review-u: neka model daje ocene pouzdanosti po polju, rutiraj ekstrakcije niske pouzdanosti ka čoveku i analiziraj tačnost po tipu dokumenta i polju radi provere dosledne tačnosti.

domains_reinforced.en: Domain 4 (Prompt Engineering & Structured Output), Domain 5 (Context Management & Reliability)
domains_reinforced.sr: Domen 4 (Prompt engineering i strukturisan izlaz), Domen 5 (Kontekst i pouzdanost)

title.en: Design and Debug a Multi-Agent Research Pipeline
title.sr: Dizajniraj i debaguj multi-agent istraživački pipeline

objective.en: Practice orchestrating subagents, managing context passing, implementing error propagation, and handling synthesis with provenance tracking.
objective.sr: Vežbaj orkestraciju subagenata, upravljanje prenosom konteksta, propagaciju grešaka i sintezu sa praćenjem provenijencije.

steps.en: Build a coordinator agent that delegates to at least two subagents (e.g., web search and document analysis). Ensure the coordinator's allowedTools includes "Task" and that each subagent receives its research findings directly in its prompt rather than relying on automatic context inheritance.
steps.sr: Napravi koordinatora koji delegira bar dvama subagentima (npr. web pretraga i analiza dokumenata). Obezbedi da allowedTools koordinatora sadrži "Task" i da svaki subagent dobije svoje nalaze direktno u promptu, bez oslanjanja na automatsko nasleđivanje konteksta.

steps.en: Implement parallel subagent execution by having the coordinator emit multiple Task tool calls in a single response. Measure the latency improvement compared to sequential execution.
steps.sr: Implementiraj paralelno izvršavanje subagenata tako što koordinator emituje više Task poziva u jednom odgovoru. Izmeri poboljšanje latencije u odnosu na sekvencijalno izvršavanje.

steps.en: Design structured output for subagents that separates content from metadata: each finding should include a claim, evidence excerpt, source URL/document name, and publication date. Verify that the synthesis subagent preserves source attribution when combining findings.
steps.sr: Dizajniraj strukturisan izlaz subagenata koji odvaja sadržaj od metapodataka: svaki nalaz sadrži tvrdnju, isečak dokaza, URL/ime dokumenta i datum objave. Proveri da subagent za sintezu čuva atribuciju izvora pri kombinovanju nalaza.

steps.en: Implement error propagation: simulate a subagent timeout and verify the coordinator receives structured error context (failure type, attempted query, partial results). Test that the coordinator can proceed with partial results and annotate the final output with coverage gaps.
steps.sr: Implementiraj propagaciju grešaka: simuliraj timeout subagenta i proveri da koordinator dobija strukturisan kontekst greške (tip otkaza, pokušani upit, parcijalni rezultati). Testiraj da koordinator može da nastavi sa parcijalnim rezultatima i anotira finalni izlaz rupama u pokrivenosti.

steps.en: Test with conflicting source data (e.g., two credible sources with different statistics) and verify the synthesis output preserves both values with source attribution rather than arbitrarily selecting one, and structures the report to distinguish well-established from contested findings.
steps.sr: Testiraj sa protivrečnim izvorima (npr. dva pouzdana izvora sa različitim statistikama) i proveri da sinteza čuva obe vrednosti sa atribucijom umesto proizvoljnog biranja jedne, i da izveštaj razdvaja dobro utvrđene od spornih nalaza.

domains_reinforced.en: Domain 1 (Agentic Architecture & Orchestration), Domain 2 (Tool Design & MCP Integration), Domain 5 (Context Management & Reliability)
domains_reinforced.sr: Domen 1 (Agentska arhitektura), Domen 2 (Dizajn alata i MCP), Domen 5 (Kontekst i pouzdanost)

title.en: Appendix
title.sr: Dodatak

title.en: Technologies and Concepts
title.sr: Tehnologije i koncepti

intro.en: The following list contains technologies and concepts that might appear on the exam:
intro.sr: Sledeća lista sadrži tehnologije i koncepte koji se mogu pojaviti na ispitu:

items.name: Claude Agent SDK
items.en: Agent definitions, agentic loops, stop_reason handling, hooks (PostToolUse, tool call interception), subagent spawning via Task tool, allowedTools configuration
items.sr: Definicije agenata, agentske petlje, obrada stop_reason-a, hook-ovi (PostToolUse, presretanje poziva alata), pokretanje subagenata preko Task alata, allowedTools konfiguracija

items.name: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
items.en: MCP servers, MCP tools, MCP resources, isError flag, tool descriptions, tool distribution, .mcp.json configuration, environment variable expansion
items.sr: MCP serveri, MCP alati, MCP resursi, isError flag, opisi alata, raspodela alata, .mcp.json konfiguracija, ekspanzija environment varijabli

items.name: Claude Code
items.en: CLAUDE.md configuration hierarchy (user/project/directory), .claude/rules/ with YAML frontmatter path-scoping, .claude/commands/ for slash commands, .claude/skills/ with SKILL.md frontmatter (context: fork, allowed-tools, argument-hint), plan mode, direct execution, /memory command, /compact, --resume, fork_session, Explore subagent
items.sr: Hijerarhija CLAUDE.md (korisnik/projekat/direktorijum), .claude/rules/ sa YAML frontmatter opsegom po putanji, .claude/commands/ za slash komande, .claude/skills/ sa SKILL.md frontmatter-om (context: fork, allowed-tools, argument-hint), plan mod, direktno izvršavanje, /memory komanda, /compact, --resume, fork_session, Explore subagent

items.name: Claude Code CLI
items.en: -p / --print flag for non-interactive mode, --output-format json, --json-schema for structured CI output
items.sr: -p / --print flag za neinteraktivni režim, --output-format json, --json-schema za strukturisan CI izlaz

items.name: Claude API
items.en: tool_use with JSON schemas, tool_choice options ("auto", "any", forced tool selection), stop_reason values ("tool_use", "end_turn"), max_tokens, system prompts
items.sr: tool_use sa JSON šemama, tool_choice opcije ("auto", "any", prinudni izbor alata), stop_reason vrednosti ("tool_use", "end_turn"), max_tokens, sistemski promptovi

items.name: Message Batches API
items.en: 50% cost savings, up to 24-hour processing window, custom_id for request/response correlation, polling for completion, no multi-turn tool calling support
items.sr: 50% uštede, prozor obrade do 24h, custom_id za uparivanje zahteva/odgovora, polling za završetak, bez podrške za višekružne pozive alata

items.name: JSON Schema
items.en: Required vs optional fields, enum types, nullable fields, "other" + detail string patterns, strict mode for syntax error elimination
items.sr: Obavezna vs opciona polja, enum tipovi, nullable polja, „other" + detail string obrasci, strict mod za eliminaciju sintaksnih grešaka

items.name: Pydantic
items.en: Schema validation, semantic validation errors, validation-retry loops
items.sr: Validacija šeme, semantičke validacione greške, validation-retry petlje

items.name: Built-in tools
items.en: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob — their purposes and selection criteria
items.sr: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob — njihove svrhe i kriterijumi izbora

items.name: Few-shot prompting
items.en: Targeted examples for ambiguous scenarios, format demonstration, generalization to novel patterns
items.sr: Ciljani primeri za dvosmislene scenarije, demonstracija formata, generalizacija na nove obrasce

items.name: Prompt chaining
items.en: Sequential task decomposition into focused passes
items.sr: Sekvencijalna dekompozicija zadatka na fokusirane prolaze

items.name: Context window management
items.en: Token budgets, progressive summarization, lost-in-the-middle effects, context extraction, scratchpad files
items.sr: Budžeti tokena, progresivno sažimanje, lost-in-the-middle efekti, ekstrakcija konteksta, scratchpad fajlovi

items.name: Session management
items.en: Session resumption, fork_session, named sessions, session context isolation
items.sr: Nastavak sesije, fork_session, imenovane sesije, izolacija konteksta sesije

items.name: Confidence scoring
items.en: Field-level confidence, calibration with labeled validation sets, stratified sampling for error rate measurement
items.sr: Pouzdanost na nivou polja, kalibracija labelovanim validacionim setovima, stratifikovano uzorkovanje za merenje stope grešaka

title.en: In-Scope Topics
title.sr: Teme u opsegu (ulaze na ispit)

intro.en: The following topics are explicitly tested on the exam:
intro.sr: Sledeće teme se eksplicitno proveravaju na ispitu:

items.name: Agentic loop implementation
items.en: Control flow based on stop_reason, tool result handling, loop termination conditions
items.sr: Kontrola toka na osnovu stop_reason-a, obrada rezultata alata, uslovi zaustavljanja petlje

items.name: Multi-agent orchestration
items.en: Coordinator-subagent patterns, task decomposition, parallel subagent execution, iterative refinement loops
items.sr: Obrasci koordinator-subagent, dekompozicija zadataka, paralelno izvršavanje subagenata, iterativne petlje dorade

items.name: Subagent context management
items.en: Explicit context passing, structured state persistence, crash recovery using manifests
items.sr: Eksplicitan prenos konteksta, strukturisano čuvanje stanja, oporavak od pada preko manifesta

items.name: Tool interface design
items.en: Writing effective tool descriptions, splitting vs consolidating tools, tool naming to reduce ambiguity
items.sr: Pisanje efektivnih opisa alata, razbijanje vs spajanje alata, imenovanje alata radi smanjenja dvosmislenosti

items.name: MCP tool and resource design
items.en: Resources for content catalogs, tools for actions, description quality for adoption
items.sr: Resursi za kataloge sadržaja, alati za akcije, kvalitet opisa radi usvajanja

items.name: MCP server configuration
items.en: Project vs user scope, environment variable expansion, multi-server simultaneous access
items.sr: Opseg projekat vs korisnik, ekspanzija environment varijabli, istovremen pristup više servera

items.name: Error handling and propagation
items.en: Structured error responses, transient vs business vs permission errors, local recovery before escalation
items.sr: Strukturisane greške, tranzijentne vs poslovne vs dozvolne greške, lokalni oporavak pre eskalacije

items.name: Escalation decision-making
items.en: Explicit criteria, honoring customer preferences, policy gap identification
items.sr: Eksplicitni kriterijumi, poštovanje želja korisnika, identifikacija rupa u politici

items.name: CLAUDE.md configuration
items.en: Hierarchy (user/project/directory), @import patterns, .claude/rules/ with glob patterns
items.sr: Hijerarhija (korisnik/projekat/direktorijum), @import obrasci, .claude/rules/ sa glob obrascima

items.name: Custom commands and skills
items.en: Project vs user scope, context: fork, allowed-tools, argument-hint frontmatter
items.sr: Opseg projekat vs korisnik, context: fork, allowed-tools, argument-hint frontmatter

items.name: Plan mode vs direct execution
items.en: Complexity assessment, architectural decisions, single-file changes
items.sr: Procena složenosti, arhitektonske odluke, izmene jednog fajla

items.name: Iterative refinement
items.en: Input/output examples, test-driven iteration, interview pattern, sequential vs parallel issue resolution
items.sr: Ulaz/izlaz primeri, iteracija vođena testovima, interview obrazac, sekvencijalno vs paralelno rešavanje problema

items.name: Structured output via tool_use
items.en: Schema design, tool_choice configuration, nullable fields to prevent hallucination
items.sr: Dizajn šeme, tool_choice konfiguracija, nullable polja radi sprečavanja halucinacija

items.name: Few-shot prompting
items.en: Ambiguous scenario targeting, format consistency, false positive reduction
items.sr: Ciljanje dvosmislenih scenarija, doslednost formata, smanjenje false positive-a

items.name: Batch processing
items.en: Message Batches API appropriateness, latency tolerance assessment, failure handling by custom_id
items.sr: Primerenost Message Batches API-ja, procena tolerancije latencije, obrada neuspeha po custom_id

items.name: Context window optimization
items.en: Trimming verbose tool outputs, structured fact extraction, position-aware input ordering
items.sr: Skraćivanje opširnih izlaza alata, ekstrakcija strukturisanih činjenica, redosled ulaza svestan pozicije

items.name: Human review workflows
items.en: Confidence calibration, stratified sampling, accuracy segmentation by document type and field
items.sr: Kalibracija pouzdanosti, stratifikovano uzorkovanje, segmentacija tačnosti po tipu dokumenta i polju

items.name: Information provenance
items.en: Claim-source mappings, temporal data handling, conflict annotation, coverage gap reporting
items.sr: Mapiranja tvrdnja–izvor, obrada vremenskih podataka, anotacija sukoba, izveštavanje o rupama u pokrivenosti

title.en: Exam Preparation Recommendations
title.sr: Preporuke za pripremu ispita

intro.en: To prepare for this certification exam:

items.en: Build an agent with the Claude Agent SDK: Implement a complete agentic loop with tool calling, error handling, and session management. Practice spawning subagents and passing context between them.
items.sr: Napravi agenta sa Claude Agent SDK-om: implementiraj kompletnu agentsku petlju sa pozivima alata, error handling-om i upravljanjem sesijom. Vežbaj pokretanje subagenata i prenos konteksta među njima.

items.en: Configure Claude Code for a real project: Set up CLAUDE.md with a configuration hierarchy, create path-specific rules in .claude/rules/, build custom skills with frontmatter options (context: fork, allowed-tools), and integrate at least one MCP server.
items.sr: Konfiguriši Claude Code za stvaran projekat: postavi CLAUDE.md sa hijerarhijom, napravi pravila po putanji u .claude/rules/, izgradi custom skills sa frontmatter opcijama (context: fork, allowed-tools) i integriši bar jedan MCP server.

items.en: Design and test MCP tools: Write tool descriptions that clearly differentiate similar tools. Implement structured error responses with error categories and retryable flags. Test tool selection reliability with ambiguous requests.
items.sr: Dizajniraj i testiraj MCP alate: piši opise koji jasno razlikuju slične alate. Implementiraj strukturisane greške sa kategorijama i retryable flag-ovima. Testiraj pouzdanost izbora alata na dvosmislenim zahtevima.

items.en: Build a structured data extraction pipeline: Use tool_use with JSON schemas, implement validation-retry loops, design schemas with optional/nullable fields, and practice batch processing with the Message Batches API.
items.sr: Napravi pipeline za ekstrakciju strukturisanih podataka: koristi tool_use sa JSON šemama, implementiraj validation-retry petlje, dizajniraj šeme sa opcionim/nullable poljima i vežbaj batch obradu Message Batches API-jem.

items.en: Practice prompt engineering techniques: Write few-shot examples for ambiguous scenarios. Define explicit review criteria to reduce false positives. Design multi-pass review architectures for large code reviews.
items.sr: Vežbaj tehnike prompt engineering-a: piši few-shot primere za dvosmislene scenarije. Definiši eksplicitne kriterijume review-a radi smanjenja false positive-a. Dizajniraj arhitekture review-a u više prolaza za velike code review-e.

items.en: Study context management patterns: Practice extracting structured facts from verbose tool outputs, implementing scratchpad files for long sessions, and designing subagent delegation to manage context limits.
items.sr: Uči obrasce upravljanja kontekstom: vežbaj izdvajanje strukturisanih činjenica iz opširnih izlaza alata, scratchpad fajlove za duge sesije i delegiranje subagentima radi upravljanja kontekst limitima.

items.en: Review escalation and human-in-the-loop patterns: Understand when to escalate (policy gaps, customer requests, inability to progress) versus resolve autonomously. Practice designing human review workflows with confidence-based routing.
items.sr: Pregledaj obrasce eskalacije i human-in-the-loop: razumi kada eskalirati (rupe u politici, zahtevi korisnika, nemogućnost napretka) a kada rešiti samostalno. Vežbaj dizajn tokova ljudskog review-a sa rutiranjem po pouzdanosti.

items.en: Complete the Practice Exam: Before sitting for the real exam, complete the practice exam (the link will be provided separately). The practice exam covers the same scenarios and question format as the real exam and shows explanations after each answer to help reinforce your understanding.
items.sr: Uradi probni ispit: pre izlaska na pravi ispit, uradi probni (link se dostavlja posebno). Probni pokriva iste scenarije i format pitanja kao pravi i prikazuje objašnjenja posle svakog odgovora radi učvršćivanja razumevanja.
